Video.summary: Kim walks us through how to create hand-shaped apples, easy for kids to follow!
Video.title: The Best Ideas For Kids: Handprint Apple

Image.alt: The Best Ideas For Kids: Handprint Apple
The Best Ideas For Kids: Handprint Apple

Video.summary: "Learn how to make a Potted Faux Flower Craft as a DIY centerpiece for springtime, bridal showers, baby showers, and more! This video tutorial shows you how to make a faux flower as well as a vase for your brand new bloom. This easy die cut craft is perfect for spring decorating, and unlike real plants, this flower craft is low maintenance and can go anywhere in your home! Be sure to watch this DIY craft tutorial for a spring craft idea you'll love. "
Video.title: FaveCrafts: Potted Faux Flower Craft

Image.alt: FaveCrafts: Potted Faux Flower Craft
FaveCrafts: Potted Faux Flower Craft

Video.summary: Learn how to make a fizzy lavender bath soak.
Video.title: FaveCrafts: Fizzy Lavender Homemade Bath Soak Recipe

Image.alt: FaveCrafts: Fizzy Lavender Homemade Bath Soak Recipe
FaveCrafts: Fizzy Lavender Homemade Bath Soak Recipe

Video.summary: A wall-hanging cabinet to help you organize and store your drill and router bits.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Bit Storage Cabinet

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Bit Storage Cabinet
The Wood Whisperer: Bit Storage Cabinet

Video.summary: Here’s a quick review of the dust and fumes that can make our lives miserable in the shop, and why we need respirators for pro-active protection. And below you will find a links to the exact respirator setup I like to use: the 3m 7500 Series.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Respirators in the Wood Shop aka Dust In Time

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Respirators in the Wood Shop aka Dust In Time
The Wood Whisperer: Respirators in the Wood Shop aka Dust In Time

Video.summary: Madonna teaches Open Lens Stars.
Video.title: Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 14 - Open Lens Stars

Image.alt: Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 14 - Open Lens Stars
Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 14 - Open Lens Stars

Video.summary: Whether you're a beginner or advanced knitter, learn and practice the Art of Knitting with the best designers and instructors in America!
Video.title: The Art of Knitting: Soul Food

Image.alt: The Art of Knitting: Soul Food
The Art of Knitting: Soul Food

Video.summary: Designing furniture can be quite tricky for new and experienced woodworkers alike. Many of us are more than capable of building just about anything, but when posed with the challenge of designing something from scratch we start shaking in our shop aprons.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Engineer the Design - Mortise and Tenon Sizing

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Engineer the Design - Mortise and Tenon Sizing
The Wood Whisperer: Engineer the Design - Mortise and Tenon Sizing

Video.summary: Yesterday, a member of our Guild Facebook group described some difficulty they were having making mortises with a hand-held router. This is something I do quite frequently and the person jested that I make it look too easy. Like anything, making mortises gets easier with practice but I believe anyone can find success with this particular process if they just follow some simple guidelines.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Quick and Easy Mortises

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Quick and Easy Mortises
The Wood Whisperer: Quick and Easy Mortises

Video.summary: There aren't usually a lot of new innovations in the world of woodworking safety gear but there are occasional improvements that can really make a practical difference in the shop. Let's take a look at a few such improvements in eye, lung, and hearing protection.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Safety Gear Upgrades

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Safety Gear Upgrades
The Wood Whisperer: Safety Gear Upgrades

Video.summary: As much as for my own curiosity as everyone else's edification, I wanted to do a side by side comparison between some popular variations of wiping varnish. I've used all of these finishes in the past but I have never used them side by side. By finishing some test boards, I figured I could not only gather information about their application characteristics but also the resulting look of the finish.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Wiping Varnish Shootout

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Wiping Varnish Shootout
The Wood Whisperer: Wiping Varnish Shootout

Video.summary: A road trip to Philip Morley's shop in Texas to watch him build his elegant kitchen table.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Modern Kitchen Table by Philip Morley

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Modern Kitchen Table by Philip Morley
The Wood Whisperer: Modern Kitchen Table by Philip Morley

Video.summary: I resaw the spalted maple panels on the bandsaw using Laguna’s Resaw King. What a bandsaw blade! I then glue up the doors and begin creating the ebony plugs for the case. I finish up by showing my process for fitting inset doors.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 11

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 11
The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 11

Video.summary: I'll show you the method I use to get a nice burnished hook on my curved and gooseneck scrapers.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: How to Sharpen a Gooseneck Scraper

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: How to Sharpen a Gooseneck Scraper
The Wood Whisperer: How to Sharpen a Gooseneck Scraper

Video.summary: As time goes on, I'm getting closer and closer to replacing every piece of furniture in my house with something I built with my own hands. A lofty goal! In this video, I show you how to make a king size bed as it's one of the bigger ticket items on that shrinking list!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: King Size Bed

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: King Size Bed
The Wood Whisperer: King Size Bed

Video.summary: Think you can’t build furniture because you only have a few basic tools? Well, check this out! This walnut low entertainment center was built using three primary tools: a circular saw, a router, and a drill. The unit has sliding doors, good ventilation, a little shelf in the back for a surge protector, and all of the trim is beveled at an angle for a more interesting visual effect
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Low Entertainment Center Part 1

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Low Entertainment Center Part 1
The Wood Whisperer: Low Entertainment Center Part 1

Video.summary: During the construction of my Split-Top Roubo Workbench, I was surprised to see Benchcrafted's plans calling for numerous tapped threads. Instead of attaching the various components of the leg vise using screws or bolts with nuts, they actually recommend cutting threads into the wood!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Tapping Threads in Wood

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Tapping Threads in Wood
The Wood Whisperer: Tapping Threads in Wood

Video.summary: We focus on the back panel system, which includes the installation of rare-earth magnets. I also show how a #7 jointer plane can be very useful in getting a perfectly straight edge on highly figured woods. To make life a little easier, I start gluing up a few sub-assemblies. And you will also see the new Final Cut Blade make an appearance.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 8

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 8
The Wood Whisperer: Gadget Station Part 8

Video.summary: This is the first in a 3-part series dedicated to the design and construction of a trestle table. But its not your everyday trestle table, since it will have a smooth sculpted look and will be used as a PC gaming desk. Made from solid Honudran Mahogany, it will only get more beautiful with time as it ages to a deep dark red color.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Trestle Table Part 1

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Trestle Table Part 1
The Wood Whisperer: Trestle Table Part 1

Video.summary: A coffee table to doubles as a gaming surface!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Gaming Coffee Table

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Gaming Coffee Table
The Wood Whisperer: Gaming Coffee Table

Video.summary: An extensive review of the most popular miter gauges on the market.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: The Ultimate Miter Gauge Review

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: The Ultimate Miter Gauge Review
The Wood Whisperer: The Ultimate Miter Gauge Review

Video.summary: This 4-In-A-Row Game is fun for the whole family and can be made with basic materials and tools. If you're not familiar with this classic game, the goal is to achieve four discs in a row while blocking your competitor from doing the same. The fact that it's oversized makes it that much more fun.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: 4-In-A-Row Game

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: 4-In-A-Row Game
The Wood Whisperer: 4-In-A-Row Game

Video.summary: A one-day build using 1/4 sheet of plywood and a 2x4.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: DIY Kids School Desk - Quick - Easy - Inexpensive!

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: DIY Kids School Desk - Quick - Easy - Inexpensive!
The Wood Whisperer: DIY Kids School Desk - Quick - Easy - Inexpensive!

Video.summary: What's up folks, for this Frog Food we are imagining Toya & Bella as Secret Agents in this moody, monochromatic vibe. So get ready for another Speed Paint and Commentary!
Video.title: Digital Painting - Frog Food: Drawing Commentary - Do you Actually NEED an Art Style

Image.alt: Digital Painting - Frog Food: Drawing Commentary - Do you Actually NEED an Art Style
Digital Painting - Frog Food: Drawing Commentary - Do you Actually NEED an Art Style

Video.summary: Madonna teaches Cluster 4 Front.
Video.title: Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 2 - Cluster 4 Front

Image.alt: Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 2 - Cluster 4 Front
Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 2 - Cluster 4 Front

Video.summary: Join DIY expert and mom, Lynn Lilly as she welcomes creatives, chefs, moms and entrepreneurs to the Craft Box Girls Studio. In today's show, Lynn creates her very own herb garden!
Video.title: Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More

Image.alt: Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More
Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More

Origami Tessellations - Advent of Tess: Day 11 - Fairy Rings

Video.summary: What’s up guys, for today’s Frog Food we’re exploring new horizons in this cool comic style piece. From inspiration to workflow we gotcha covered, so sit back, relax, and enjoy.
Video.title: Digital Painting - Frog Food: Attempting Comics - Speed Paint

Image.alt: Digital Painting - Frog Food: Attempting Comics - Speed Paint
Digital Painting - Frog Food: Attempting Comics - Speed Paint

Video.summary: Let's create long from art foamies in this Art Foamies 101 class! Craft Kathy Cano-Murillo!

Image.alt: The Crafty Chica: Art Foamies 101
The Crafty Chica: Art Foamies 101

Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: First Day of School Memories
